metadata.description: The location for all resources. Defaults to resource group location.
The size of the VM. Defaults to Standard_B2s.
The SKU of the SQL Database. Defaults to Basic.
IP Address range allowed for administrative access. CIDR format (e.g., 203.0.113.0/24).
Admin username for VM SSH access.
URL of the Redmine installation script. Must be publicly accessible.
Enable WAF on Application Gateway for production deployments.
Retention days for Log Analytics diagnostics.

properties.description: Allow HTTP from App Gateway subnet only
Allow SSH from admin IP range only
Deny all other inbound traffic (Zero Trust default)
Allow HTTP to AzureCloud for package updates
Allow HTTPS to AzureCloud for secure updates
Allow inbound HTTP traffic from Internet (port 80)
Allow inbound HTTPS traffic from Internet (port 443)
Allow Azure Gateway Manager health probes
[format('Redmine OpenID Connect integration for {0} environment', parameters('environmentName'))]

properties.scriptContent:
#!/bin/bash
      set -euo pipefail
      
      echo "[$(date)] Starting secret generation script..."
      
      # Function to check if secret exists
      secret_exists() {
        az keyvault secret show --vault-name "$KEYVAULT_NAME" --name "$1" &>/dev/null && echo "true" || echo "false"
      }
      
      # Function to store secret (idempotent)
      store_secret() {
        local secret_name=$1
        local secret_value=$2
        
        if [ "$(secret_exists "$secret_name")" = "true" ]; then
          echo "[$(date)] Secret '$secret_name' already exists. Skipping."
          return 0
        fi
        
        if az keyvault secret set \
          --vault-name "$KEYVAULT_NAME" \
          --name "$secret_name" \
          --value "$secret_value" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "[$(date)] ✓ Created secret: $secret_name"
        else
          echo "[$(date)] ✗ Failed to create secret: $secret_name" >&2
          return 1
        fi
      }
      
      echo "[$(date)] === Generating Application Secrets ==="
      
      # 1. SQL Admin Password (32 chars, complex)
      echo "[$(date)] Generating SQL admin password..."
      SQL_PASSWORD=$(openssl rand -base64 32 | tr -d "=+/" | cut -c1-25)
      store_secret "sql-admin-password" "$SQL_PASSWORD"
      
      # 2. Redmine Secret Key (Rails RAILS_MASTER_KEY format preferred, 32 bytes hex)
      echo "[$(date)] Generating Redmine secret key..."
      REDMINE_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
      store_secret "redmine-secret-key" "$REDMINE_SECRET"
      
      # 3. SSH Private Key (ED25519 format - modern, secure)
      echo "[$(date)] Generating SSH key pair..."
      ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /tmp/id_ed25519 -N "" -C "redmine-admin@azure" 2>/dev/null || true
      SSH_PRIVATE_KEY=$(cat /tmp/id_ed25519 | base64 -w0)
      SSH_PUBLIC_KEY=$(cat /tmp/id_ed25519.pub)
      store_secret "ssh-private-key" "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY"
      store_secret "ssh-public-key" "$SSH_PUBLIC_KEY"
      
      # 4. Entra Client Secret (32 char alphanumeric)
      echo "[$(date)] Generating Entra client secret..."
      ENTRA_SECRET=$(openssl rand -base64 24 | tr -d "=+/")
      store_secret "entra-client-secret" "$ENTRA_SECRET"
      
      # 5. Tenant ID (from context)
      echo "[$(date)] Retrieving Tenant ID..."
      TENANT_ID=$(az account show --query tenantId -o tsv)
      store_secret "tenant-id" "$TENANT_ID"
      
      echo "[$(date)] === All secrets generated and stored successfully ==="
      echo "[$(date)] ✓ sql-admin-password"
      echo "[$(date)] ✓ redmine-secret-key"
      echo "[$(date)] ✓ ssh-private-key (base64 encoded)"
      echo "[$(date)] ✓ ssh-public-key"
      echo "[$(date)] ✓ entra-client-secret"
      echo "[$(date)] ✓ tenant-id"

#!/bin/bash
      set -euo pipefail
      
      echo "[$(date)] Configuring Entra App Registration Client Secret"
      
      # Retrieve the auto-generated secret from Key Vault
      echo "[$(date)] Retrieving secret from Key Vault..."
      ENTRA_SECRET=$(az keyvault secret show \
        --vault-name "$KEYVAULT_NAME" \
        --name "entra-client-secret" \
        --query value -o tsv) || {
        echo "[$(date)] Error: Failed to retrieve secret from Key Vault" >&2
        exit 1
      }
      
      # NOTE: Creating credentials in Entra ID requires elevated Graph permissions
      # The secret is generated and stored in Key Vault
      # To use it in Entra: manually add it via Azure Portal or use credentials with Graph.Application permission
      echo "[$(date)] ✓ Entra client secret stored in Key Vault: entra-client-secret"
      echo "[$(date)] NOTE: Manual configuration may be required in Entra ID if Graph permissions are limited"
      
      # Store the Application ID in Key Vault for VM access
      echo "[$(date)] Storing Application ID in Key Vault..."
      az keyvault secret set \
        --vault-name "$KEYVAULT_NAME" \
        --name "entra-client-id" \
        --value "$APP_ID" > /dev/null || {
        echo "[$(date)] Warning: Failed to store client ID in Key Vault" >&2
      }
      
      echo "[$(date)] ✓ Entra configuration completed"

#!/bin/bash
      set -euo pipefail
      
      echo "[$(date)] === Generating Self-Signed Certificate for Application Gateway ==="
      
      # Check if certificate already exists
      if az keyvault secret show --vault-name "$KEYVAULT_NAME" --name "$CERT_SECRET_NAME" &>/dev/null; then
        echo "[$(date)] Certificate already exists. Skipping generation."
        exit 0
      fi
      
      # Generate private key and self-signed certificate
      # Valid for 1 year, can be replaced with real cert later
      echo "[$(date)] Generating RSA private key and self-signed certificate..."
      openssl req \
        -x509 \
        -newkey rsa:2048 \
        -keyout /tmp/server.key \
        -out /tmp/server.crt \
        -days 365 \
        -nodes \
        -subj "/CN=$DOMAIN_NAME/O=RedmineDeploy/C=US" 2>/dev/null || {
        echo "[$(date)] Error: Failed to generate certificate" >&2
        exit 1
      }
      
      # Create PKCS#12 format (required by App Gateway)
      echo "[$(date)] Creating PKCS#12 format..."
      openssl pkcs12 \
        -export \
        -in /tmp/server.crt \
        -inkey /tmp/server.key \
        -out /tmp/server.pfx \
        -name "appgw-cert" \
        -passout pass:"" 2>/dev/null || {
        echo "[$(date)] Error: Failed to create PKCS#12 format" >&2
        exit 1
      }
      
      # Base64 encode the PFX for Key Vault storage
      echo "[$(date)] Encoding certificate for Key Vault..."
      PFX_BASE64=$(cat /tmp/server.pfx | base64 -w0)
      
      # Store in Key Vault (as secret because Key Vault has native cert support)
      echo "[$(date)] Storing certificate in Key Vault..."
      az keyvault secret set \
        --vault-name "$KEYVAULT_NAME" \
        --name "$CERT_SECRET_NAME" \
        --value "$PFX_BASE64" > /dev/null || {
        echo "[$(date)] Error: Failed to store certificate in Key Vault" >&2
        exit 1
      }
      
      echo "[$(date)] ✓ Self-signed certificate generated and stored successfully"
      
      # Cleanup temp files
      rm -f /tmp/server.key /tmp/server.crt /tmp/server.pfx
      echo "[$(date)] Cleanup completed"

value.message: Deployment completed successfully. All secrets are stored in Key Vault.

value.nextSteps: Download certificates and keys from Key Vault. Never share or commit to source control.
